Decontam

Gabriela Puerres

2025-07-02

Contaminant Identification with Decontam

R package ‘decontam’ version 1.26.0

1. Setting

Load Required Libraries

library(decontam)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(phyloseq)
library(ggpubr)
library(pairwiseAdonis)
library(patchwork)
library(microbiome)

Define Sample Types and Paths

TYPE <- c("Feces")
# Define subdirectories
path.rds      <- file.path( "Analysis", "RDS")
path.figures  <- file.path( "Analysis", "Figures")

Load Phyloseq Object

ps_d <- readRDS(file.path(path.rds, "phyloseq.rds"))
ps_d
## phyloseq-class experiment-level object
## otu_table()   OTU Table:         [ 24011 taxa and 164 samples ]
## sample_data() Sample Data:       [ 164 samples by 56 sample variables ]
## tax_table()   Taxonomy Table:    [ 24011 taxa by 7 taxonomic ranks ]
#Check the tax_table aspect of the phyloseq object:
colnames(tax_table(ps_d))
## [1] "Kingdom" "Phylum"  "Class"   "Order"   "Family"  "Genus"   "Species"
tax_df <- as.data.frame(tax_table(ps_d))

Remove Mock Communities from this phyloseq object and add the new ones previously analized

# Remove old Mock Communities Data:
ps_d_no_mock <- subset_samples(ps_d, Sample_Type != "Mock")
ps_d_no_mock
## phyloseq-class experiment-level object
## otu_table()   OTU Table:         [ 24011 taxa and 157 samples ]
## sample_data() Sample Data:       [ 157 samples by 56 sample variables ]
## tax_table()   Taxonomy Table:    [ 24011 taxa by 7 taxonomic ranks ]
# Load New Mock Communities Data:
ps_d_mock <- readRDS(file.path(path.rds, "ps.mock.merged.rds"))
ps_d_mock
## phyloseq-class experiment-level object
## otu_table()   OTU Table:         [ 1137 taxa and 5 samples ]
## sample_data() Sample Data:       [ 5 samples by 97 sample variables ]
## tax_table()   Taxonomy Table:    [ 1137 taxa by 7 taxonomic ranks ]
# Add New Mock Communities Data:
ps_d_mock_merged <- merge_phyloseq(ps_d_no_mock, ps_d_mock)
ps_d_mock_merged #162 samples: feces, controls (+/-)
## phyloseq-class experiment-level object
## otu_table()   OTU Table:         [ 24176 taxa and 162 samples ]
## sample_data() Sample Data:       [ 162 samples by 97 sample variables ]
## tax_table()   Taxonomy Table:    [ 24176 taxa by 7 taxonomic ranks ]
# update the metada:
meta_from_csv <- read.csv("Analysis/Meta/Final_Cow_FecalMicrobiome_Metadata.csv", row.names = 1, check.names = FALSE)
# Check metadata and Ps have the same name
all(rownames(meta_from_csv) %in% sample_names(ps_d_mock_merged))
## [1] TRUE
# Assign new metadata to phyloseq object
sample_data(ps_d_mock_merged) <- sample_data(meta_from_csv)

# Save the merged object that does not have the Mock group (Mock_C)
saveRDS(ps_d_mock_merged, file.path(path.rds, "ps_d_mock_merged.rds"))

#Check the tax_table aspect of the phyloseq object:
colnames(tax_table(ps_d_mock_merged))
## [1] "Kingdom" "Phylum"  "Class"   "Order"   "Family"  "Genus"   "Species"
tax_df <- as.data.frame(tax_table(ps_d_mock_merged))

Prepare Metadata

sample_data(ps_d_mock_merged)$Sample_Type[sample_data(ps_d_mock_merged)$Sample_Type == "Neither"] <- "Sample"


#remove the commas and convert the qpcr_16s_copies_ul column to numeric format in R
sample_data(ps_d_mock_merged)$qpcr_16s_copies_ul <- as.numeric(
  gsub(",", "", sample_data(ps_d_mock_merged)$qpcr_16s_copies_ul)
)

sample_data(ps_d_mock_merged)$qpcr_16s_copies_ul <- as.numeric(sample_data(ps_d_mock_merged)$qpcr_16s_copies_ul)
# Change the name of the Mock_communities:
# Convert sample data to a data.frame (if needed)
metadata <- as.data.frame(sample_data(ps_d_mock_merged))

# Replace specific Sample_Type entries
metadata$Sample_Type[metadata$Sample_Type %in% c("Mock_ZYMO_D6323", "Mock_ZYMO_D6324", "Mock_ZYMO_D6325")] <- "Mock_A"
metadata$Sample_Type[metadata$Sample_Type %in% c("Mock_ZYMO_D6311", "Mock_ZYMO_D6312")] <- "Mock_B"

# Reassign the updated metadata back to the phyloseq object
sample_data(ps_d_mock_merged) <- metadata

2. Frequency-Based Contaminant Detection

Prepare data for ‘frequency’ method: Subset Ps objects

# 1. No Mock samples: phyloseq object without positive controls
exclude_mock <- c("Mock_A", "Mock_B")
ps.samples.d.wo.mock <- subset_samples(ps_d_mock_merged, !Sample_Type %in% exclude_mock) 
ps.samples.d.wo.mock <- prune_taxa(taxa_sums(ps.samples.d.wo.mock) > 0, ps.samples.d.wo.mock) # remove taxa with no counts
ps.samples.d.wo.mock
## phyloseq-class experiment-level object
## otu_table()   OTU Table:         [ 22236 taxa and 157 samples ]
## sample_data() Sample Data:       [ 157 samples by 97 sample variables ]
## tax_table()   Taxonomy Table:    [ 22236 taxa by 7 taxonomic ranks ]
# 2. Positive Samples: phyloseq object without negative controls
exclude_controls <- c("Environmental_Control", "Extraction_Control", "Reagent_Control")
ps.samples.d.wo.controls <- subset_samples(ps_d_mock_merged, !Sample_Type %in% exclude_controls) 
ps.samples.d.wo.controls <- prune_taxa(taxa_sums(ps.samples.d.wo.controls) > 0, ps.samples.d.wo.controls) # remove taxa with no counts
ps.samples.d.wo.controls
## phyloseq-class experiment-level object
## otu_table()   OTU Table:         [ 22185 taxa and 147 samples ]
## sample_data() Sample Data:       [ 147 samples by 97 sample variables ]
## tax_table()   Taxonomy Table:    [ 22185 taxa by 7 taxonomic ranks ]
# 3. Only True Samples = "Feces"
exclude_mock <- c("Mock_A", "Mock_B")
ps.samples.d.wo.controls.mock <- subset_samples(ps.samples.d.wo.controls, !Sample_Type %in% exclude_mock) 
ps.samples.d.wo.controls.mock <- prune_taxa(taxa_sums(ps.samples.d.wo.controls.mock) > 0, ps.samples.d.wo.controls.mock) # remove taxa with no counts
ps.samples.d.wo.controls.mock #Phyloseq clean without positive and negative samples!
## phyloseq-class experiment-level object
## otu_table()   OTU Table:         [ 21086 taxa and 142 samples ]
## sample_data() Sample Data:       [ 142 samples by 97 sample variables ]
## tax_table()   Taxonomy Table:    [ 21086 taxa by 7 taxonomic ranks ]

Run frequency method for only true samples “Feces”

# Run frequency method
contamdf.freq <- isContaminant(ps.samples.d.wo.controls.mock, method="frequency", conc="qpcr_16s_copies_ul", threshold = 0.5) # DNA concentration is stored in the qpcr_16s_copies_ul column
table(contamdf.freq$contaminant)
## 
## FALSE  TRUE 
## 19166  1920
# Display the number of sequence features classified as contaminants
# FALSE: Number of sequence features not classified as contaminants
# TRUE: Number of sequence features 
contam_feature_indices <- which(contamdf.freq$contaminant == TRUE)
head(contam_feature_indices)
## [1]   6   7  97 120 125 154
# Vector of sequence feature indices in otu table that are contaminants
# Select contaminants from otu table
contam_seq_freq <- rownames(contamdf.freq[contamdf.freq$contaminant == "TRUE",])

contamdf.freqMod <- contamdf.freq %>%
 filter(prev > 1) %>%
 mutate(Prevalence = ifelse(prev == 2, "2",
 ifelse(prev > 2 & prev <= 5, "3-5",
 ifelse(prev >= 6 & prev <= 10, "6-10", "11+"))) )

contamdf.freqMod$Prevalence <- factor(contamdf.freqMod$Prevalence, levels = c("2", "3-5", "6-10", "11+"))

Plot score statistics output from ‘frequency’ method

# Set up:
theme_set(theme_bw())
 # Part of the Area Green Tableau color palette (Version 9.x)
 # Similar to Davis et al., (2018)
scaleColorFillManualFrequency <-
 scale_fill_manual(
 values =
 c(
 "2" = "#dbe8b4",
 "3-5" = "#9ad26d",
 "6-10" = "#6cae59",
 "11+" = "#4a8c1c"
 )
 )
# Plot score statistics output
freqScorePlot <- ggplot(contamdf.freqMod, aes(x = p, fill = Prevalence)) +
 geom_histogram(bins = 30) +
 scaleColorFillManualFrequency +
 ggtitle("Frequency Method") +
 xlab("Score Statistic") +
 ylab("Frequency") +
 theme(
 plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
 legend.position = "none")
print(freqScorePlot)

3. Prevalence-Based Contaminant Detection

Prevalence method for Sample + Mock + Controls

# Prepare data for 'prevalence' method. 
sample_data(ps_d_mock_merged)$is.neg <- sample_data(ps_d_mock_merged)$Sample_Type %in% c("Environmental_Control", "Extraction_Control", "Reagent_Control")
# Run 'prevalence' method
# Information about whether the sample ir a control or true sample is stored in 'is.neg' variable
contamdf.prev <- isContaminant(ps_d_mock_merged, method="prevalence", neg="is.neg", threshold=0.5)
table(contamdf.prev$contaminant)
## 
## FALSE  TRUE 
## 23706   470
# Display the number of sequence features classified as contaminants
# FALSE: Number of sequence features not classified as contaminants
# TRUE: Number of sequence features classified as contaminants
contam_feature_indices_prev <- which(contamdf.prev$contaminant == TRUE)
head(contam_feature_indices_prev)
## [1]  827 1669 2131 2755 2846 3091
# 827 1669 2131 2755 2846 3091
# Vector of sequence feature indices in otu table that are contaminants
# Make phyloseq object of presence-absence in negative controls and true samples (Feces and Mock)
ps.pa <- transform_sample_counts(
  ps_d_mock_merged, 
  function(abund) 1*(abund>0))

ps.pa.neg <- prune_samples(
  sample_data(ps.pa)$Sample_Type %in% c(
    "Environmental_Control",
    "Extraction_Control", 
    "Reagent_Control"), 
  ps.pa)

ps.pa.pos <- prune_samples(
  sample_data(
    ps.pa)$Sample_Type == c(
      "Feces", "Mock_A", "Mock_B"), ps.pa)

# Make data.frame of prevalence in positive and negative samples
df.pa <- data.frame(
  pa.pos=taxa_sums(ps.pa.pos), 
  pa.neg=taxa_sums(ps.pa.neg),
  contaminant=contamdf.prev$contaminant)

pa.plot <- ggplot(data=df.pa, 
                  aes(x=pa.neg, 
                      y=pa.pos, 
                      color=contaminant)) + geom_point() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")+
  xlab("Prevalence (Negative Controls) 
      Environmental Control, Extraction Control, Reagent Control") +
  ylab("Prevalence (True Samples)
       Feces + Mock_A + Mock_B") +
  ggtitle("Prevalence Plot: 
          Sample + Mock + Control")
pa.plot

Prevalence method for Sample + Controls

# Make phyloseq object of presence-absence in negative controls and true samples (Feces)
ps.pa.wo.mock <- transform_sample_counts(
  ps_d_mock_merged, 
  function(abund) 1*(abund>0))

ps.pa.neg.wo.mock <- prune_samples(
  sample_data(ps.pa)$Sample_Type %in% c(
    "Environmental_Control",
    "Extraction_Control",
    "Reagent_Control"), 
  ps.pa.wo.mock)

ps.pa.pos.wo.mock <- prune_samples(
  sample_data(
    ps.pa.wo.mock)$Sample_Type == c(
      "Feces"), ps.pa.wo.mock)

# Make data.frame of prevalence in positive and negative samples
df.pa.wo.mock <- data.frame(
  pa.pos.wo.mock=taxa_sums(ps.pa.pos.wo.mock), 
  pa.neg.wo.mock=taxa_sums(ps.pa.neg.wo.mock),
  contaminant=contamdf.prev$contaminant)

pa.plot.wo.mock <- ggplot(
  data=df.pa.wo.mock, 
  aes(x=pa.neg.wo.mock, 
      y=pa.pos.wo.mock, 
      color=contaminant)) +
  geom_point() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")+
  xlab("Prevalence (Negative Controls) 
      Environmental Control, Extraction Control, Reagent Control") +
  ylab("Prevalence (True Samples)
       Feces") +
  ggtitle("Prevalence Plot: 
          Sample + Control")
pa.plot.wo.mock

Prevalence-Based Detection: Extraction Control

#Prepare data for 'prevalence' method, "Extraction control" is control

ps.no.extraction <- subset_samples(ps_d_mock_merged, Sample_Type != "Extraction_Control") # remove extraction samples
ps.no.extraction <- prune_taxa(taxa_sums(ps.no.extraction) > 0, ps.no.extraction) # remove taxa with no counts
ps.no.extraction
## phyloseq-class experiment-level object
## otu_table()   OTU Table:         [ 23034 taxa and 157 samples ]
## sample_data() Sample Data:       [ 157 samples by 98 sample variables ]
## tax_table()   Taxonomy Table:    [ 23034 taxa by 7 taxonomic ranks ]
# Run 'prevalence' method
# Information about whether the sample ir a control or true sample is stored in 'is.neg' variable

contamdf.prev.extraction <- isContaminant(ps.no.extraction, 
                                          method="prevalence", 
                                          neg="is.neg", 
                                          threshold=0.5)

table(contamdf.prev.extraction$contaminant)
## 
## FALSE  TRUE 
## 22675   359
# FALSE: Number of sequence features not classified as contaminants
# TRUE: Number of sequence features classified as contaminants

# Identify contaminant feature indices:
contam_feature_indices_prev_extraction <- which(
  contamdf.prev.extraction$contaminant == TRUE)

head(contam_feature_indices_prev_extraction) 
## [1] 2108 2732 2823 3068 3359 3558
# Vector of sequece feature indices in otu table that are contaminants
# Note: Some of the most abundant sequence features were classified as contaminants
# Plot Extraction Control Score
contamdf.prev.extractionMod <- contamdf.prev.extraction %>%
 filter(prev > 1) %>%
 mutate(Prevalence = ifelse(prev == 2, "2",
 ifelse(prev > 2 & prev <= 5, "3-5",
 ifelse(prev >= 6 & prev <= 10, "6-10", "11+")))
 )


contamdf.prev.extractionMod$Prevalence <- factor(
  contamdf.prev.extractionMod$Prevalence, 
  levels = c("2", "3-5", "6-10", "11+"))


prev.ExtractionScorePlot <- ggplot(
  contamdf.prev.extractionMod, 
  aes(x = p, fill = Prevalence)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30, color = "black") +
  scaleColorFillManualFrequency +
  ggtitle("Prevalence Method 
         (Extraction Control)") +
  xlab("Score Statistic") +
  ylab("Frequency") +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.title.y = element_blank()
    )

prev.ExtractionScorePlot

#select seq names of contaminants identified by prevalence method using extraction as control
contam_seq_prev_extraction <- rownames(contamdf.prev.extraction[contamdf.prev.extraction$contaminant == "TRUE",])

Prevalence-Based Detection: Reagent Control

#Prepare data for 'prevalence' method, "Reagent control" is control

ps.no.reagent <- subset_samples(ps_d_mock_merged, Sample_Type != "Reagent_Control") # remove extraction samples
ps.no.reagent <- prune_taxa(taxa_sums(ps.no.reagent) > 0, ps.no.reagent) # remove taxa with no counts
ps.no.reagent
## phyloseq-class experiment-level object
## otu_table()   OTU Table:         [ 22959 taxa and 157 samples ]
## sample_data() Sample Data:       [ 157 samples by 98 sample variables ]
## tax_table()   Taxonomy Table:    [ 22959 taxa by 7 taxonomic ranks ]
# Run 'prevalence' method
# Information about whether the sample ir a control or true sample is stored in 'is.neg' variable

contamdf.prev.reagent <- isContaminant(ps.no.reagent, method="prevalence", neg="is.neg", threshold=0.5)

table(contamdf.prev.reagent$contaminant)
## 
## FALSE  TRUE 
## 22620   339
# FALSE: Number of sequence features not classified as contaminants
# TRUE: Number of sequence features classified as contaminants

# Identify contaminant feature indices:
contam_feature_indices_prev_reagent <- which(contamdf.prev.reagent$contaminant == TRUE)
head(contam_feature_indices_prev_reagent) 
## [1]  813 1651 2108 2823 3024 3068
# Vector of sequece feature indices in otu table that are contaminants
# Note: Some of the most abundant sequence features were classified as contaminants
# Plot Reagent Control Score

# Filter to keep only taxa with prevalence > 1 and categorize prevalence into bins
contamdf.prev.reagentMod <- contamdf.prev.reagent %>%
 filter(prev > 1) %>%
 mutate(Prevalence = ifelse(prev == 2, "2",
                            ifelse(prev > 2 & prev <= 5, "3-5",
                                   ifelse(prev >= 6 & prev <= 10, "6-10", "11+")))
 )

# Make sure the prevalence factor has the right order
contamdf.prev.reagentMod$Prevalence <- factor(
  contamdf.prev.reagentMod$Prevalence, 
  levels = c("2", "3-5", "6-10", "11+"))

# Plot histogram of the score statistic 'p' colored by prevalence category
prev.reagentScorePlot <- ggplot(
  contamdf.prev.reagentMod, 
  aes(x = p, fill = Prevalence)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30, color = "black") +
  scaleColorFillManualFrequency +
  ggtitle("Prevalence Method 
         (Reagent Control)") +
  xlab("Score Statistic") +
  ylab("Frequency") +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.title.y = element_blank()
 )
prev.reagentScorePlot

#select seq names of contaminants identified by prevalence method using reagent as control
contam_seq_prev_reagent <- rownames(contamdf.prev.reagent[contamdf.prev.reagent$contaminant == "TRUE",])

Prevalence-Based Detection: Environment Control

#Prepare data for 'prevalence' method, without "Extraction control" is control 
ps.no.Environment  <- subset_samples(ps_d_mock_merged, Sample_Type != "Environmental_Control")  # remove extraction samples 

ps.no.Environment  <- prune_taxa(taxa_sums(ps.no.Environment) > 0, ps.no.Environment) # remove taxa with no counts 
ps.no.Environment
## phyloseq-class experiment-level object
## otu_table()   OTU Table:         [ 22881 taxa and 157 samples ]
## sample_data() Sample Data:       [ 157 samples by 98 sample variables ]
## tax_table()   Taxonomy Table:    [ 22881 taxa by 7 taxonomic ranks ]
# Run 'prevalence' method
# Information about whether the sample ir a control or true sample is stored in 'is.neg' variable  

contamdf.prev.Environment <- isContaminant(
  ps.no.Environment, 
  method="prevalence", 
  neg="is.neg", 
  threshold=0.5)  

table(contamdf.prev.Environment$contaminant)  
## 
## FALSE  TRUE 
## 22555   326
# FALSE: Number of sequence features not classified as contaminants 
# TRUE: Number of sequence features classified as contaminants  


# Identify contaminant feature indices:
contam_feature_indices_prev_Environment <- which(contamdf.prev.Environment$contaminant == TRUE) 

head(contam_feature_indices_prev_Environment)  
## [1]  813 1651 2108 2732 2823 3024
# Vector of sequece feature indices in otu table that are contaminants 
# Plot Environment Control Score 

# Filter to keep only taxa with prevalence > 1 and categorize prevalence into bins
contamdf.prev.EnvironmentMod <- contamdf.prev.Environment %>%
 filter(prev > 1) %>%
 mutate(Prevalence = ifelse(prev == 2, "2",
                            ifelse(prev > 2 & prev <= 5, "3-5",
                                   ifelse(prev >= 6 & prev <= 10, "6-10", "11+")))
 )

# Make sure the prevalence factor has the right order
contamdf.prev.EnvironmentMod$Prevalence <- factor(
  contamdf.prev.EnvironmentMod$Prevalence, 
  levels = c("2", "3-5", "6-10", "11+"))

# Plot histogram of the score statistic 'p' colored by prevalence category
prev.EnvironmentScorePlot <- ggplot(
  contamdf.prev.EnvironmentMod, 
  aes(x = p, fill = Prevalence)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30, color = "black") +
  scaleColorFillManualFrequency +
  ggtitle("Prevalence Method 
         (Environmental Control)") +
  xlab("Score Statistic") +
  ylab("Frequency") +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.title.y = element_blank()
 )
prev.EnvironmentScorePlot

#select seq names of contaminants identified by prevalence method using environmental as control
contam_seq_prev_Environment <- rownames(contamdf.prev.Environment[contamdf.prev.Environment$contaminant == "TRUE",])

4. Phyloseq object after Decontam

# Create noncontam ps project
# Select unique seq names of contaminants identified by both frequency and prevalence methods separately
contam_seq <- c(contam_seq_freq,
                contam_seq_prev_reagent,
                contam_seq_prev_extraction,
                contam_seq_prev_Environment)

contam_seq <- unique(contam_seq)
# Create ps project after removing contaminants
allTaxa = taxa_names(ps_d_mock_merged)
Taxa.decontam <- allTaxa[!(allTaxa %in% contam_seq)]
ps.decontam <- prune_taxa(Taxa.decontam, ps_d_mock_merged)
saveRDS(ps.decontam, file.path(path.rds, "ps.decontam.rds"))

# Create ps project of the contaminants
ps.contam <- prune_taxa(contam_seq, ps_d_mock_merged)
saveRDS(ps.contam, file.path(path.rds, "ps.contam.rds"))

# Look inside the ps object
ps.decontam
## phyloseq-class experiment-level object
## otu_table()   OTU Table:         [ 21818 taxa and 162 samples ]
## sample_data() Sample Data:       [ 162 samples by 98 sample variables ]
## tax_table()   Taxonomy Table:    [ 21818 taxa by 7 taxonomic ranks ]
ps.contam
## phyloseq-class experiment-level object
## otu_table()   OTU Table:         [ 2358 taxa and 162 samples ]
## sample_data() Sample Data:       [ 162 samples by 98 sample variables ]
## tax_table()   Taxonomy Table:    [ 2358 taxa by 7 taxonomic ranks ]

Plots

Plot 16S Gene Copy vs Abundance of Contaminants

SAMPLE_TYPES <- c(
 "Feces",
 "Mock_A",
 "Mock_B",
 "Extraction_Control", 
 "Reagent_Control",
 "Environmental_Control")

my_palette<- c(
 "Feces" = "#1f77b4",
 "Mock_A" = "#9f7f3f",
 "Mock_B" = "#7f4f7f",
 "Extraction_Control" = "#ff7f0e",
 "Reagent_Control" = "#2ca02c",
 "Environmental_Control" = "#d62728")
#Data
readRDS(file.path(path.rds, "ps.decontam.rds"))
## phyloseq-class experiment-level object
## otu_table()   OTU Table:         [ 21818 taxa and 162 samples ]
## sample_data() Sample Data:       [ 162 samples by 98 sample variables ]
## tax_table()   Taxonomy Table:    [ 21818 taxa by 7 taxonomic ranks ]
readRDS(file.path(path.rds, "ps.contam.rds"))
## phyloseq-class experiment-level object
## otu_table()   OTU Table:         [ 2358 taxa and 162 samples ]
## sample_data() Sample Data:       [ 162 samples by 98 sample variables ]
## tax_table()   Taxonomy Table:    [ 2358 taxa by 7 taxonomic ranks ]
metadata_plot <- data.frame(sample_data(ps.contam))

#Subset Mock groups:
metadata_plot$Mock_Group <- NA  # initialize column
metadata_plot$Mock_Group[metadata_plot$Sample_ID %in% c("ZYMO-1", "ZYMO-2", "ZYMO-3")] <- "Mock_A"
metadata_plot$Mock_Group[metadata_plot$Sample_ID %in% c("ZYMO-4", "ZYMO-5")] <- "Mock_B"


# Updata data:
metadata_plot$Sample_Type[metadata_plot$Sample_Type == "Mock"] <- 
  metadata_plot$Mock_Group[metadata_plot$Sample_Type == "Mock"]


# Set up the data for the plot
metadata_plot$Sample_ID <- rownames(metadata_plot) 
counts <- data.frame(sample_sums(ps.contam))
colnames(counts) <- "counts"
counts$Sample_ID <- rownames(counts)
metadata_plot <- as.data.frame(left_join(metadata_plot, counts, by = "Sample_ID"))
metadata_plot$Sample_Type <- factor(metadata_plot$Sample_Type, levels =  SAMPLE_TYPES)

# Plot all the samples and controls
p.contam.counts <- ggplot(data = metadata_plot, aes(x = log(qpcr_16s_copies_ul), y = counts / 1000)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = Sample_Type), size = 2) +
  geom_smooth(aes(group = 1), method = "lm", se = TRUE) +
  stat_cor(label.x = 2, label.y = 700, size = 3) +
  stat_regline_equation(label.x = 2, label.y = 650, size = 3) +
  xlab("Log (16S gene copy number)") +
  ylab("Counts / 1000") +
  scale_color_manual(values = my_palette)

p.contam.counts # 16S gene copy number vs abundance of contaminants
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

# Plot for the samples and controls, except Mock_B
metadata_plot_wo.MockB <- metadata_plot %>% filter(Sample_Type != "Mock_B")
p.contam.counts.wo.MockB <- ggplot(data = metadata_plot_wo.MockB,
                                   aes(x = log(qpcr_16s_copies_ul), 
                                       y = log(counts/100))) +
  geom_point(aes(color = Sample_Type), size = 2) +
  geom_smooth(aes(group = 1), method = "lm", se = TRUE) +
  stat_cor(label.x = 2, label.y = 15, size = 3) +
  stat_regline_equation(label.x = 2, label.y = 20, size = 3) +
  xlab("Log (16S gene copy number)") +
  ylab("Log (Counts/100)") +
  ylim(0, 25) +
  scale_color_manual(values = my_palette)

p.contam.counts.wo.MockB 
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

# Box plot without Mock B

p.contam.counts.wo.MockB.BP <-  ggplot(metadata_plot_wo.MockB,
                           aes(x = Sample_Type, y = counts / 100, fill = Sample_Type)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.6, outlier.shape = NA) +
  geom_jitter(aes(color = Sample_Type), width = 0.2, size = 1.5) +
  ylab("Counts / 100") +
  xlab("Sample Type") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = my_palette) +
  scale_color_manual(values = my_palette) +
  theme_minimal()

# Print plot
p.contam.counts.wo.MockB.BP + 
  ylim(0, 400) +  
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) + 
  scale_x_discrete(expand = expansion(mult = c(0.05, 0.1))) 

# Density Plot
# Visualize distribution of contaminants across copy numbers.
ggplot(metadata_plot, aes(x = log(qpcr_16s_copies_ul), fill = Sample_Type)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = my_palette) +
  xlab("Log (16S gene copy number)") +
  theme_minimal()

# Facet by Sample Type 
# See the relationship per group without overplotting

ggplot(metadata_plot, aes(x = log(qpcr_16s_copies_ul), y = log(counts / 100))) +
  geom_point(color = "gray40", size = 2) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "black") +
  facet_wrap(~ Sample_Type) +
  xlab("Log (16S gene copy number)") +
  ylab("Log (Counts/100)") +
  theme_minimal()
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

#LOESS instead of linear, to better capture curves in the data.
# LOESS can adapt to small local trends even with high variability 

ggplot(metadata_plot, aes(x = log(qpcr_16s_copies_ul), y = log(counts / 100), color = Sample_Type)) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = TRUE,  span = 0.7) +
  scale_color_manual(values = my_palette) +
  xlab("Log (16S gene copy number)") +
  ylab("Log (Counts/100)") +
  theme_minimal()
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

Overlay linear and LOESS model

ggplot(metadata_plot, aes(x = log(qpcr_16s_copies_ul), y = log(counts / 100), color = Sample_Type)) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  
  # LOESS curve (dashed line)
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = FALSE, linetype = "dashed", size = 1) +

  # Linear model (solid black line)
  geom_smooth(aes(group = 1), method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "black", size = 1) +

  scale_color_manual(values = my_palette) +
  xlab("Log (16S gene copy number)") +
  ylab("Log (Counts / 100)") +
  theme_minimal() +
  ggtitle("16S Gene Copy vs Contaminant Abundance: LOESS + Linear Fit")
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

#Dashed lines: Nonlinear local trends (LOESS) per group.

#Solid black line: Global linear trend across all data.

Plot the Abundance of Contaminants

# Data
detection <- sum(taxa_sums(ps.contam))/162*0.05
pseq.contam <- aggregate_rare(ps.contam, 
                              level="Genus", 
                              detection = detection,
                              prevalence = 0.05)

melt.contam <- psmelt(pseq.contam)
# Update the names of the Mock samples:
melt.contam$Sample_Type <- case_when(
  melt.contam$Sample_ID %in% c("ZYMO-1", "ZYMO-2", "ZYMO-3") ~ "Mock_A",
  melt.contam$Sample_ID %in% c("ZYMO-4", "ZYMO-5") ~ "Mock_B",
  TRUE ~ melt.contam$Sample_Type) # keep other types as they are
# Plot for Feces
p.contam1 <- ggplot(
  melt.contam %>% filter(Sample_Type == "Feces"), 
  aes(x = Sample_ID, 
      y = Abundance, 
      fill = Genus)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  facet_grid(. ~ Sample_Type, 
             scales = "free_x", 
             space = "free") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks = element_blank(),
    legend.key.size = unit(10, "pt"),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank()
  ) +
  scale_fill_brewer("Genus", palette = "Paired")
p.contam1

# Plot for Mock Communities 
p.contam3 <- ggplot(
  melt.contam %>% filter(Sample_Type == c( "Mock_A", 
                                           "Mock_B")),
  aes(x = Sample_ID, y = Abundance, fill = Genus)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  facet_grid(. ~ Sample_Type, 
             scales = "free_x", 
             space = "free") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust = 1, size = 7), 
    axis.ticks = element_blank(),
    legend.key.size = unit(10, "pt"),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank()
  ) +
  scale_fill_brewer("Genus", palette = "Paired")
p.contam3

# Plot for control samples 


# Plot for Negative Controls
p.contam2 <- ggplot(
  melt.contam %>% filter(Sample_Type == c("Environmental_Control",
    "Extraction_Control", "Reagent_Control")),
  aes(x = Sample_ID, y = Abundance, fill = Genus)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  facet_grid(. ~ Sample_Type, 
             scales = "free_x", 
             space = "free") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x =  element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust = 1, size = 7), 
    axis.ticks = element_blank(),
    legend.key.size = unit(10, "pt"),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank()
  ) +
  scale_fill_brewer("Genus", palette = "Paired")
p.contam2

NMDS of Contaminants

## UPDATE THE NAMES OF THE MOCK SAMPLES: 
# Convert sample_data to data.frame
sample_df <- data.frame(sample_data(ps.contam))

# Update Sample_Type based on Sample_ID
sample_df$Sample_Type <- case_when(
  sample_df$Corrected_Sample_ID %in% c("ZYMO-1", "ZYMO-2", "ZYMO-3") ~ "Mock_A",
  sample_df$Corrected_Sample_ID %in% c("ZYMO-4", "ZYMO-5") ~ "Mock_B",
  TRUE ~ sample_df$Sample_Type  # keep others unchanged
)

# Replace the sample data in ps.contam
sample_data(ps.contam) <- sample_data(sample_df)


# Subset only Feces samples and Positive controls
ps.contam.sample <- subset_samples(ps.contam, 
                                   Sample_Type %in% c("Feces",
                                                      "Mock_A",
                                                      "Mock_B" ))


ps.contam.sample <- prune_taxa(taxa_sums(ps.contam)>0, 
                               ps.contam.sample)

type_colors <- c("Feces" = "#1f77b4", 
                 "Mock_A" = "#2ca02c",
                 "Mock_B"= "#d62728")


ord.bray.nmds.contam <- ordinate(ps.contam.sample, 
                                 "NMDS", 
                                 "bray", 
                                 k=4, 
                                 trymax = 1000)
## Square root transformation
## Wisconsin double standardization

## ... Similar to previous best
## *** Best solution repeated 1 times
ord.pnmds.contam <- plot_ordination(ps.contam.sample, ord.bray.nmds.contam, color = "Sample_Type") +
 geom_point(size = 3.0) +
 theme_bw() +
 scale_color_manual(values = type_colors) +
 theme(axis.text = element_text(size = 12),
 axis.title = element_text(size = 14),
 plot.title = element_text(hjust=0.5))+
 annotate("text", x = -1, y = 2, label = "Stress = 0.162\nPERMANOVA P = 0.001",
 hjust = 0)
print(ord.pnmds.contam) # NMDS of contaminants

PCoA of contaminants

ps.pcoa <- transform_sample_counts(ps.contam.sample, function(x) log1p(x))
ord.pcoa <- ordinate(ps.pcoa, method = "PCoA", distance = "bray")


# Plot
plot_ordination(ps.pcoa, ord.pcoa, color = "Sample_Type") +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  scale_color_manual(values = type_colors) +
  theme_minimal() +
  ggtitle("PCoA (Bray-Curtis) on log-transformed ASV table")

PERMANOVA

# Make sure it lists at least two groups with more than one sample each

bray.dist.contam<-vegdist(otu_table(ps.contam.sample), method='bray')
metadata.sample <- sample_data(ps.contam.sample) %>% data.frame()
beta_div.contam <-pairwise.adonis2(bray.dist.contam ~ Sample_Type, 
                                   data=metadata.sample, 
                                   permutations=999)
## 'nperm' >= set of all permutations: complete enumeration.
## Set of permutations < 'minperm'. Generating entire set.
beta_div.contam
## $parent_call
## [1] "bray.dist.contam ~ Sample_Type , strata = Null , permutations 999"
## 
## $Feces_vs_Mock_A
##           Df SumOfSqs      R2      F Pr(>F)    
## Model      1    1.836 0.03387 5.0131  0.001 ***
## Residual 143   52.376 0.96613                  
## Total    144   54.212 1.00000                  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## $Feces_vs_Mock_B
##           Df SumOfSqs      R2      F Pr(>F)    
## Model      1    1.145 0.02136 3.0989  0.001 ***
## Residual 142   52.468 0.97864                  
## Total    143   53.613 1.00000                  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## $Mock_A_vs_Mock_B
##          Df SumOfSqs      R2      F Pr(>F)
## Model     1  1.13530 0.91095 30.689    0.1
## Residual  3  0.11098 0.08905              
## Total     4  1.24628 1.00000              
## 
## attr(,"class")
## [1] "pwadstrata" "list"

Combine the Plots
